untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about backtesting methodology and sample validation, not a tradeable technical condition. It requires tracking historical sample statistics (minimum 100 market moves with directional split validation) and system performance metrics across collected data. These are backtesting framework requirements, not indicator-based entry/exit conditions. The signal describes pre-trading validation steps rather than real-time price/indicator thresholds that can be expressed with allowed indicators and operators.
translation_notes: THARP_DRY_12_0 is a portfolio construction and due diligence rule from 'Trade Your Way to Financial Freedom'. It mandates: (1) select timeframe, (2) collect ≥100 market samples, (3) ensure ≥45% up moves AND ≥45% down moves (max 10% skew), (4) backtest system against collected moves, (5) only then deploy live. This is a framework constraint, not a signal expression. Implementation requires custom backtesting logic to validate sample adequacy before any position entry rules apply.
